creator: crest@rlwinm.de
text: Rerooting isn't a full replacement because it requires the kernel and its initial root file system to come from somewhere other than the runtime root file system. With isboot you can boot from iSCSI with iPXE (either flashed into the NIC or chainloaded) keeping the system (kernel and userland) on s single iSCSI target under full control of the iSCSI initiator.
